title: Fix podman python docker-py tests
body:
* Refactor packaging so unittest discovery works
* Refactor tests to use python3-docker.rpm that ships with Fedora32
* Flush image cache between tests suites
* Update documentation to reflect changes

Outstanding issue:
* client.get_image() does not fail if image does not exist

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@jwhonce, thank you for the cleanup. py test much-needed this pass.
Just, not a biggie. Having a test folder again under the main test folder might not be needed, discover would work even if the dir name would be prefixed `test`. Hence I named dir  test_docker_py. And Simply `PYTHONPATH=/usr/bin/python python -m unittest discover .` under libpod/test should work
Ignore the comment if dir is needed for the better maintenance

Also, I have a failed attempt at updating the readme here #6759. @edsantiago the readme here looks better and addresses most of your comments. We can have this in and I rebase and address any remaining comments.
